Closes #

### Context
<!--
Why does this change exist? Link the issue, incident, or decision that
motivated it. One or two sentences. A reviewer
should understand the reason without opening the diff.
-->

### Changes
<!--
Describe the change at the level of decisions, not lines - the reviewer
can already see the lines. What approach was taken, and why this approach
over the obvious alternatives? If the diff is large, point at where the
interesting part lives (e.g. "the real change is in emails.py; the rest
is renames").
-->

### Scope boundaries
<!--
What was deliberately NOT done, and where is it tracked? This records
omissions as decisions rather than oversights, and preempts
"why didn't you also..." review comments. Delete this section if the
change has no meaningful boundary.
-->

### Testing done
<!--
Concrete evidence, not "tested locally". What did you actually run?
Which acceptance criteria were verified, including failure paths?
Screenshots for UI changes, command output or test names for behaviour.
A reviewer should be able to repeat your verification from this section.
-->

### Risk, rollout, and reviewer guidance
<!--
Only where relevant - delete otherwise. Migrations? Backwards
compatibility? Feature flags? How to roll back if this goes wrong?
Anything the reviewer should look at extra hard, or can safely skim?
-->
